title: ulmus × hollandica var. klemmeri
intro: Ulmus × hollandica var. klemmeri, also known as Klemmer's elm, is a cultivar of the Dutch elm tree, primarily used for its medicinal and ornamental purposes. The plant's bark and leaves contain compounds with potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Klemmer's elm has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant's herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
